question_text: What happens to light when it enters a denser medium, such as light passing from air into glass?

options.label: A
options.text: The light speeds up and bends away from the normal

options.label: B
options.text: The light slows down and bends towards the normal

options.label: C
options.text: The light continues at the same speed and does not bend

options.label: D
options.text: The light speeds up and bends towards the normal

correct_answer: The light slows down and bends towards the normal
explanation: When light travels from a less dense medium (air) into a denser medium (glass), it slows down. This change in speed causes the light to change direction, bending towards the normal line (an imaginary line perpendicular to the surface). This phenomenon is called refraction.
